description: PR-9 learner-state engine logic review (companion to pr9-learner-state.md).
method: adversarial audit of cc_spine/learner_state.py by (a) an operator self-review, then (b) an INDEPENDENT agent reviewer (author != reviewer) that ran the engine end-to-end and cross-referenced the quiz/holdout/registry stores.
process_note: The independent review completed AFTER the initial PR-9 commit (8a49deb) — a repeat of the PR-7 commit-before-review gap. Its four minor findings were fixed in PR-9.1; no blockers or majors. Lesson reinforced: wait for the independent review before committing.

findings.problem: non-total sort key let same-item/same-time attempts depend on input order (determinism hole)
graded['graded'] unchecked — an ungradable attempt was silently scored wrong instead of failing closed
findings.fix: raise LearnerStateError when graded['graded'] is false

findings.problem: journal recorded the first distractor's misconception, not the selected choice's (latent: all items currently single-misconception)
findings.fix: use the selected distractor's misconception from quiz.grade; progress all item misconceptions on a correct answer

findings.problem: rejection relied only on holdout.json membership; a holdout-flagged item leaked into practice would pass
findings.fix: also raise when the resolved item's holdout flag is true (defense in depth)
